# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a simple Dockerfile for our Go microservice. Compile the application and run the binary. Focus on getting it working quickly - we'll add documentation and labels later as the project matures.

Prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Golang runtime image for our example, based off Alpine Linux (alpine). Update with your needs when using a different OS or version of Go/Golang in the future. 
FROM golang:1.16-alpine3.9 AS builder   # Use an official GOLANG image for building and testing, then use it as base to create our final images (final build will be lightweight)   
WORKDIR /app     # Set working directory in the container 
COPY go.* ./      # Copy .go files from your host machine into dockerfile. This is where we'll add any dependencies or source code of Go application  
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -a -installsuffix cgo -o main .       # Build the binary with 'cgo'. We disable it to prevent container security issues  (https://www.thebuildingsproject.com/2018-building-securely/)
FROM alpine:latest   # Use an official ALPINE image as our final base in order not include unnecessary files and dependencies that we don't need, then update the working directory to /app  (final build will be lightweight)   
WORKDIR /app     # Set new Working Directory for your application. This is where you want Dockerfile location on host machine  
COPY --from=builder /usr/local/bin .      # Copy final binary from builder image into the current working directory  (final build will be lightweight)   
CMD ["./main"]     # Set default command to run when container is started. This should match your CMD in Dockerfile
